explanation.rule: These suffixes form nouns expressing beliefs and ideologies (-ism), people who follow them (-ist), or abstract qualities (-ness).

groups.note: Refers to a belief, theory, or practice

examples.definition: a philosophy centred on human values and reason
the pursuit of noble or perfect goals

groups.note: Refers to a person who holds a belief or practices an art

examples.definition: a person guided by ideals rather than practical considerations

groups.note: Forms abstract nouns from adjectives describing a quality or state

examples.definition: the state of having no meaning or purpose

wordForms.definition: a philosophy centred on human values, reason, and dignity
wordForms.example: Humanism places human welfare at the centre of ethical thinking.
wordForms.rootDefinition: of, relating to, or characteristic of people

wordForms.definition: an advocate or follower of humanism
wordForms.example: As a humanist, she believed in secular ethics over religious doctrine.
wordForms.rootDefinition: of, relating to, or characteristic of people

wordForms.definition: a tendency to oppose change; belief in traditional values
wordForms.example: Political conservatism often favours gradual rather than radical change.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to protect or preserve from loss or harm

wordForms.definition: the belief in and pursuit of noble or perfect principles
wordForms.example: The young politician's idealism inspired many voters.
wordForms.rootDefinition: a perfect example; a standard of excellence

wordForms.definition: a person guided by ideals rather than realistic thinking
wordForms.example: She was called an idealist for believing peace was achievable.
wordForms.rootDefinition: a perfect example; a standard of excellence

wordForms.definition: a person who is independent and self-reliant
wordForms.example: He was a true individualist who refused to follow trends.
wordForms.rootDefinition: a single person, considered separately from a group

wordForms.definition: the quality of being different from others
wordForms.example: The school encouraged pupils to express their individuality.
wordForms.rootDefinition: a single person, considered separately from a group

wordForms.definition: cleverness and skill in achieving something
wordForms.example: The artfulness of his argument left his opponents with nothing to say.
wordForms.rootDefinition: the expression of creativity and imagination

wordForms.example: The child's artlessness was charming and refreshing.
wordForms.rootDefinition: the expression of creativity and imagination

wordForms.definition: the state of having no meaning or purpose
wordForms.example: He felt a sense of meaninglessness after losing his job.

wordForms.definition: the absence of social class divisions
wordForms.example: Some argue that pop culture promotes a sense of classlessness.
wordForms.rootDefinition: a group of people with similar social or economic status

wordForms.definition: the state of being unable to rest or keep still
wordForms.example: His restlessness made it hard to concentrate during the lesson.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to relax or stop working for a period

multipleChoiceExercises.sentence: Her political views reflected a deep commitment to ___.
As a confirmed ___, she preferred to work independently on all projects.
The ___ of his philosophical views was what made him stand out.
The ___ of the new era contrasted with the rigid hierarchies of the past.
She was moved by the ___ of the child's paintings.
The professor's lecture touched on ___ as an alternative to religious belief.

transformExercises.sentence: She approached the problem with great ___, finding a clever solution. (ART)
His commitment to ___ meant he always acted on principles rather than profit. (IDEAL)
The philosopher was a well-known ___ who believed in rational ethics. (HUMAN)
Growing ___ among the students led to disruption in several classes. (REST)
The architect prized ___ in design, avoiding unnecessary decoration. (ART)
The ___ of modern life often causes people to search for deeper purpose. (MEAN)
